label: Hospitals Damaged
note: Iran: 25 hospitals damaged / 9 out of service (NPR); 309 health facilities + 42 ambulances damaged (IRCS); 7 hospitals evacuated — Lebanon: 61+ WHO-verified attacks on healthcare since Mar 2 (~2.9/day), 91+ medical workers killed, 14 hospitals damaged, 6 hospitals + 55 primary care centers closed, 100+ paramedics/health workers dead per MoPH — Bushehr no new strikes since Apr 4 — ICRC cross-border shipment from Türkiye Apr 14 — French UNIFIL CWO killed Apr 18 in Hezbollah attack (3 wounded)
source: Iranian Red Crescent / Iranian Health Ministry / WHO / OCHA / Lebanese MoPH / ICRC / WAFA / Al Jazeera
detail: Iran infrastructure: 115,000+ civilian buildings damaged; 309 health facilities + 42 ambulances damaged (IRCS); 7 Iranian hospitals evacuated; 700+ educational facilities damaged, 30+ universities struck; 120+ historical sites reported damaged; partial power blackouts confirmed in Tehran and Karaj by Iran's energy minister; Pasteur Institute (Iran's premier public health facility, inoperable), Khatam-al-Anbia and Gandhi hospitals damaged; Delaram Sina Psychiatric Hospital, Tofigh Daru pharmaceutical, Shahid Mutahhari Hospital destroyed. Bushehr NPP: no new strikes Apr 16-17; Apr 4 projectile strike (4th incident) killed one physical-protection staffer and damaged an auxiliary building — no radiation increase. Iran submitted incidents via INFCIRC/1353 Apr 9. IAEA DG Grossi remains 'deeply concerned' and continues invoking the 'Seven Indispensable Pillars.' AEOI head Eslami refuses inspections of struck facilities pending codified protocols for 'internationally protected facilities subjected to military attacks.' Mahshahr Petrochemical Zone struck Apr 4 (5 killed, 170 injured). All Iranian ports blockaded since Apr 13; Iran's National Petrochemical Co suspended all petrochemical exports 'until further notice' Apr 15. ICRC delivered its first medical shipment into Iran Apr 12 — first since war start. Lebanon (Lebanese MoPH via WAFA/Al Jazeera Apr 17): 2,167-2,196 killed / 7,061 wounded; 172 children, 250+ women, 93 health workers killed. WHO Lebanon: 133 attacks on healthcare; 80+ health-worker deaths; 55 primary care centers and 6 hospitals closed; 14 hospitals damaged. OCHA Lebanon flash appeal seeks $308.3M through May. UNHCR reports 202,477+ cross-border flows from Lebanon into Syria since Mar 2 (incl. 27,343 Lebanese nationals). UNICEF Rapid Response Mechanism in Lebanon has reached 188,155 IDPs across 360+ shelters. Qaaqaiyet el Jisr and Delaffi bridges plus Litani crossings destroyed — isolating the south. 472 schools repurposed as shelters. Sultaniye power station damaged; water pipelines in Marjaayoun hit. Battle of Bint Jbeil: IDF 98th Division consolidated the sector — Apr 16 Egoz Unit raid on Hezbollah 'combat compound' in Aynata casbah seized RPGs, Kalashnikovs, grenades, an anti-aircraft missile, and surveillance gear; IDF demolished ~70 Hezbollah infrastructure sites in under a minute via coordinated detonation. Separately IDF recovered 130+ weapons stashed in a Bint Jbeil school alongside Hezbollah flags. Cumulative Bint Jbeil toll: 100+ Hezbollah fighters killed. IDF strike on Nabatieh Apr 16. Lebanon ceasefire effective 5:00 PM ET Apr 16; tens of thousands of IDPs began moving southward Apr 17 despite IDF spokesman Adraee's warning not to return south of the Litani yet. WHO Flash Appeal $30M for 5 most-affected countries (incl. $10M for Lebanon). WHO Lebanon mobilized 60+ tonnes of medical supplies since Mar 18 via EU Humanitarian Airbridge. Displacement: Lebanon >1.2M internal (~20% of population); Iran up to 3.2M IDPs (UNHCR Mar 12, likely stale); 4.4M Afghan refugees at elevated risk. Source: WHO / IRCS / IAEA / OCHA / UNHCR / HRANA / UNICEF / Hengaw / NBC News / Lebanese MoPH / WAFA / Al Jazeera / ICRC.

label: Schools Destroyed
note: 481 Iranian schools damaged (USCRI); 700+ educational facilities affected; 65+ schools destroyed per OCHA. 30+ universities struck including Shahid Beheshti University (Apr 3), Sharif University AI data center. Minab school (165-180 killed). 115,000+ civilian buildings damaged. 130+ historical/cultural heritage sites damaged incl. Golestan Palace and Isfahan's Naqsh-e Jahan Square (UNESCO). Rafi-Nia Synagogue in central Tehran destroyed in adjacent strike during Passover. Lebanon: IDF recovered 130+ weapons stashed in a Bint Jbeil school alongside Hezbollah flags Apr 16. IRGC threatened retaliatory strikes on US/Israeli campuses in Qatar and UAE.
source: Iranian Red Crescent / AP / NYT / Amnesty
detail: Girls' school struck by US Tomahawk cruise missile adjacent to IRGC naval compound. Amnesty International verified 168 killed (100+ schoolchildren aged 7-12) at Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school on February 28. US internal investigation (Mar 11) determined the strike was likely due to outdated DIA intelligence — the school was once part of an IRGC compound but had been walled off since 2016. Weapons experts confirmed Tomahawk missile fragments. UN investigation initiated March 17, ongoing. HRW and Amnesty demanded accountability for potential war crimes.

label: Power Plants Hit
detail: Grid disruptions across western Iran. Isfahan, Kermanshah, Ahvaz power infrastructure degraded. Civilian blackouts reported in multiple provinces.

label: Oil/Gas Facilities Hit
note: IEA: 44+ energy assets severely damaged across 9 countries + Kharg Island 50+ targets Apr 7 + Asaluyeh (South Pars, ~50% of petrochemical production) — worst since combined 1973 + 1979 oil shocks
source: IEA / DoD / Bloomberg / QatarEnergy / Reuters / satellite imagery
detail: IEA (Mar 23): At least 44 energy assets severely damaged across 9 countries — 'very severe, worse than the combined 1973 and 1979 oil shocks.' IEA released record 400M barrels from strategic stockpiles (US: 172M from SPR). Iraq declared force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields (production collapsed from 4.2M to ~1.2-1.4M bpd). Combined Gulf production cuts exceed 10M bpd (JPMorgan). Iran: Kharg destroyed, South Pars Phases 3/4/5/6 struck. Qatar: Ras Laffan 12.8 MTPA lost (17% of LNG exports, 3-5yr repair). SAMREF (Saudi) struck. Kuwait: Mina al-Ahmadi hit. Haifa BAZAN refinery. Bahrain Bapco under force majeure. Iran sanctions lifted on ~140M barrels at sea (30 days). Jones Act waived 60 days.

label: Nuclear Facilities Struck
note: Fordow, Isfahan UCF, Natanz (3x), Bushehr (4x), Arak Heavy Water, Ardakan yellowcake (struck again Apr 7 — Shahid Rezayee Nejad facility), Yazd, Khondab (severely damaged, no longer operational) — IAEA zero access — Grossi: 'cannot rule out radiological release necessitating evacuation of areas as large as major cities'
detail: Fordow enrichment facility struck Day 1 with B-2/GBU-57 MOPs — assessed inoperable. Isfahan UCF struck Day 1. Natanz struck 4 times — underground FEP 'severely damaged.' Day 29: US-Israeli airstrikes hit Ardakan yellowcake and Khondab Heavy Water Complex. Bushehr struck 4 times — most recently Apr 4 when 1 physical protection staff killed, nearest strike impacted 75m from site perimeter. IAEA DG Grossi: strikes could cause 'severe radiological accident with consequences beyond Iran's borders.' Rosatom evacuated 198 Russian staff from Bushehr Apr 4. Iran possesses 440.9 kg of 60%-enriched uranium — enough for approximately 10 nuclear weapons if further enriched. IAEA has zero access to enrichment facilities. Grossi: 'I do not believe that Iran's nuclear program can be eliminated solely by military action.' Source: IAEA / Rosatom / Pentagon / Bulletin of Atomic Scientists.

label: Airports Damaged
note: Iran + region — 3 Tehran airports struck Apr 7 (Bahram, Mehrabad, Azmayesh) targeting air force aircraft and helicopters
detail: Mehrabad (Tehran), Shiraz TAB 6, Tabriz TAB 2 runways cratered in first wave. Dubai International Airport struck by drone Mar 16. April 7: strikes on three Tehran airports (Bahram, Mehrabad, Azmayesh) targeting Iranian air force aircraft and helicopters. Regional airspace closures affect 52,000+ commercial flights.

label: Bridges Destroyed
note: Israel launched wide-scale bridge/railway strikes Apr 7 — Qom bridge, Kashan railway bridge (2 killed, 3 injured), Tabriz-Zanjan freeway, Karaj railway — IDF issued 12-hour Farsi warning to avoid trains — all Litani River bridges destroyed in Lebanon (150,000 cut off)
source: Satellite imagery / IDF / Lebanese media / Iranian state media
detail: April 7: IDF launched 'wide-scale wave of strikes targeting dozens of infrastructure sites' — confirmed hits on key bridge near Qom, railway bridge in Kashan (2 killed, 3 injured), Tabriz-Zanjan freeway, railway infrastructure in Karaj. IDF issued 'urgent warning' in Farsi to stay away from trains/railways for 12 hours. Appears to be opening phase of Trump's 'Bridge Day.' Previous bridges destroyed in Kermanshah, Lorestan, Khuzestan provinces. Lebanon: All Litani River bridges destroyed — 150,000 people cut off in southern Lebanon. IDF permanent buffer zone south of Litani.
